original_hypothesis.title: ConfigStorage-driven dependency swapping can brick locking/unlocking if dependencies are set to non-contract or reverting addresses
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: configUpdated() repoints critical dependencies (accountManager, migrationManager, nftOverlord, snuggeryManager) based solely on configStorage values. If configStorage is compromised or misconfigured, LockManager can be bricked (e.g., accountManager.forceHarvest or getPlayer reverts) or can grant unintended behavior (e.g., migrationManager set incorrectly changes NFT minting path).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Not a pure code bug under normal trust assumptions, but a high-impact system wiring surface. Because LockManager has no sanity checks (code size, interface support), a bad update can halt user flows or change reward/NFT entitlement logic.
deployment_assumptions: ConfigStorage authority is compromised, misconfigured, or can be updated to incorrect addresses
No additional guards exist in BaseBlastManager/ConfigStorage to prevent invalid address updates
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Only scripts shown are generic deploy/config; no evidence of on-chain validation in LockManager for dependency addresses.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _reconfigure loads addresses from configStorage with no validation; lock/unlock depend on accountManager.forceHarvest/getPlayer

preconditions: configStorage updates addresses to invalid/reverting targets
onlyConfigStorage gate can be exercised (by intended authority or compromise)
Users call lock/unlock which invokes external dependency methods

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Mutable dependency injection without contract/interface sanity checks
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Protocol can be bricked or behavior changed by wrong dependency address
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No code.length checks; no try/catch; no version/interface validation

analysis_questions: Does ConfigStorage enforce code-hash allowlists or contract checks?
Is there an emergency rollback mechanism if a bad config update is made?
Are these dependencies upgradeable/proxy-based and can their behavior change unexpectedly?
validation_requirements: Review IConfigStorage and BaseBlastManager implementations for validation
Simulate configUpdated to EOA address; confirm lock/unlock revert on external calls
Assess operational procedures around config updates

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If config authority is compromised, attacker sets accountManager to a reverting contract, freezing all lock/unlock operations (funds stuck until config is repaired).

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Protocol-wide freeze of user funds (operational outage)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Medium (depends on ability to restore correct config quickly)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests for configUpdated sanity and dependency correctness checks

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: At time of config update and subsequent user actions
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: On-chain: sudden widespread reverts after configUpdated events

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
